salary.title: 2026 APAC Cybersecurity Salary Explorer
salary.standfirst: Verified salary ranges across six APAC markets, six roles, and three experience levels. Sourced from public job boards, government workforce reports, and industry surveys — updated quarterly.
salary.noResults: No roles match your filters. Try a different market or role.
salary.certTableTitle: Certification ROI: cost vs. salary boost
salary.certTableStandfirst: Head-to-head comparisons of the certs that actually move salaries in this region. Cost figures are official exam fees; boosts come from market wage data.
salary.methodologyP1: Salary ranges represent the 25th–75th percentile of public job listings combined with verified candidate compensation data, expressed in source-country annual currency.
salary.methodologyP2: Markets are bucketed by major hiring center (e.g. China T1 = Beijing, Shanghai, Shenzhen). Cross-market comparisons normalize to USD using April 2026 reference rates.
salary.methodologyP3: Source attribution is included on every record. Where a single source is named, the figure was triangulated against at least one secondary source before publication.
salary.newsletterDesc: We refresh this dataset every quarter and add new markets as we cover them. No spam — one email per release.
salary.heroCeilingDesc: Top of market for the senior tier across the dataset.
salary.heroEntryDesc: Best entry-level base salary for a 0-2 year analyst.
salary.heroSpreadDesc: How much the senior median changes across markets.
salary.mapStandfirst: Senior-band median (USD equivalent) across six APAC markets. Bigger dot = higher senior pay. Click a city to filter the cards below.
salary.heroBody: The 2026 APAC cybersecurity salary guide — 32 data points across six markets, real employer names, honest cert ROI. No paywall, no tricks, no recruiter spam.
salary.metaMarket_sg_title: Singapore Cybersecurity Salary Guide 2026 — SOC, Pentest, CISO
salary.metaMarket_sg_desc: Singapore cybersecurity pay bands — SOC analyst entry S$42k, senior engineer S$150k, CISO S$400-600k. Sourced from NodeFlair + JobStreet + ISC2 workforce data.
salary.metaMarket_my_title: Malaysia Cybersecurity Salary Guide 2026 — Kuala Lumpur Pay Data
salary.metaMarket_my_desc: Malaysia cyber pay — entry MYR 36-60k, senior MYR 130-200k. Real KL employer names, honest cert ROI, no recruiter spin. 2026 data refresh.
salary.metaMarket_cn-t1_title: China T1 Cybersecurity Salary 2026 — Beijing / Shanghai / Shenzhen
salary.metaMarket_cn-t1_desc: China Tier-1 city cyber pay — junior ¥200k, senior ¥550k-1M, finance sector premium ¥800k+. Sourced from Maimai + verified 2026 reports.
salary.metaMarket_cn-t2_title: China T2 Cybersecurity Salary 2026 — Chengdu / Hangzhou / Wuhan
salary.metaMarket_cn-t2_desc: China Tier-2 city cyber pay benchmarks and career ladder — typically 30-40% below T1 with major quality-of-life upside.
salary.metaMarket_au_title: Australia Cybersecurity Salary Guide 2026 — Sydney / Melbourne / Canberra
salary.metaMarket_au_desc: Australia cyber pay — graduate AUD 75k, senior AUD 150-200k, CISO AUD 300k+. ACS workforce data + Seek benchmarks. 2026 refresh.
salary.metaMarket_hk_title: Hong Kong Cybersecurity Salary Guide 2026 — CISO HK$2M, SOC HK$420k
salary.metaMarket_hk_desc: Hong Kong cyber pay — junior HK$264k, senior HK$1M, bank CISO HK$2M+. Direct industry intel + Robert Half data. Tax rate ~15% makes net pay elite.
salary.faq_q1: Where does the salary data come from?
salary.faq_a1: Primary sources include NodeFlair, JobStreet, Seek, JobsDB, Maimai, the ACS Workforce Report, and the ISC2 Cybersecurity Workforce Study. For Hong Kong, we also cross-check with direct industry intelligence from security professionals working at major banks. Every record carries a visible source attribution.
salary.faq_q2: Why show the salary in local currency instead of USD?
salary.faq_a2: Local currency is what you will actually be paid and what recruiters advertise. The USD equivalent is shown as a secondary comparison so you can compare markets, but the headline number matches what appears on the job posting. This prevents the confusion of seeing one number on a forum post and a different one on our site.
salary.faq_q3: What does the senior-band number actually mean?
salary.faq_a3: Senior-band is the median total cash compensation for engineers with 6-9 years of experience. It excludes sign-on bonuses and equity. For roles where top-of-market outliers (regional CISO, principal consultant) are significantly higher, we show a separate Top Earners row with sourced context — never blended into the median.
salary.faq_q4: Is the Hong Kong CISO salary really HKD 2 million?
salary.faq_a4: For in-house CISOs at major Hong Kong banks (HSBC, Standard Chartered, HKEX-level institutions), yes — the base salary is approximately HKD 2 million, with total comp reaching HKD 2.5-3.5M including bonus. For regional CISO roles at multinationals or Big 4 ex-partners, the range extends to HKD 4M+. This is sourced from direct industry intelligence; verify with Robert Half HK Salary Guide for the published benchmarks.
salary.faq_q5: Which certification gives the best ROI?
salary.faq_a5: It depends on your market and career stage. For Hong Kong and Singapore finance-sector roles, CISSP has the strongest salary signal. For offensive security roles in any market, OSCP beats CISSP. For early-career analysts in Malaysia or China Tier-2 cities, Security+ or CEH delivers acceptable ROI at much lower cost. See the cert ROI table above for the full cost-vs-boost comparison.
salary.faq_q6: How often is this data refreshed?
salary.faq_a6: Base salary bands are refreshed quarterly; top-of-market and executive records are refreshed as we receive direct intelligence. The last-updated date on the page header is authoritative. If you have newer data from your market, reply to the newsletter and we will review it.

sources.metaDescription: The full list of cybersecurity feeds, vendor research blogs, and national CERT advisories we monitor — with tier labels and honest notes on what we've excluded and why.
sources.heroSubtitle: Security pros already subscribe to ten feeds in Feedly. Our job is not to publish faster than them — it is to read all of it, throw out the noise, and surface what matters. This page shows exactly what we read, so you can judge our curation for yourself.
sources.heroCounts: We track {total} sources today: {active} active, {review} under review, and {excluded} we have deliberately excluded.
sources.sectionActiveDescription: The feeds that currently drive our daily coverage. Trusted sources carry the most editorial weight; standard sources are read for breadth and cross-confirmation.
sources.sectionReviewDescription: On probation. We are monitoring feed stability, editorial signal, and overlap with other sources before confirming long-term inclusion or exclusion.
sources.sectionExcludedDescription: Deliberately not carried. Listed here so you can verify what we read — and what we don't.
sources.suggestTitle: Not seeing a source you trust?
sources.suggestBody: Email us the name, URL, and one sentence on why it belongs here. We read every message and re-review the list quarterly. We use email on purpose — no web forms, no submission queues, no public suggestion box to game.
sources.suggestReplyHint: Replies typically within a few business days. No auto-responders.
